pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk through the house and it feels like i'm just a ghost wandering around. there's no point to any of it, honestly. i wake up, i eat, i sleep... but the person i was supposed to be with isn't here. i feel like half of me just died with them and what's left is just... empty.

pool.baseline: i can't stop thinking about how nothing actually matters. like, if you look at the scale of everything, the universe is just expanding into nothingness and eventually everything just dies. so what's the point of even trying? all the struggle, the pain, the work... it's just a flash of light before total darkness. it feels like such a waste to even exist.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i'm still doing this. the lab results came back again and it's just... nothing. another year of work down the drain. my funding is basically gone and i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this research. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to sleep and not have to wake up to this pressure ever again.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the mirror and see not a woman, but a ghost already haunting her own life. There is no more music in the world, only this endless, grey ache. I feel as though I am drifting toward a great, dark sea, and I find myself wishing I could just... let the tide take me. To simply cease to be, amidst the velvet and the shadows.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here in this apartment and realize that if i just... stopped existing tomorrow, the world wouldn't even stutter. like, i'm just a ghost in a city where nobody knows my name. it feels like i'm just taking up space that doesn't even belong to me.

pool.baseline: i lost everything. the company is gone, the liquidators are calling every hour, and the debt is... i dont even know how to describe it. it's more than i can ever pay back. i just keep thinking about my family seeing me like this. the shame is actually physical. i can't face them. i can't face anyone. it feels like there's no version of the future where i'm not a complete disgrace.
